name: Microbiome Composition Modulation Surveillance

scope.question: What changed for Microbiome Composition Modulation since the last meaningful review or publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Surveillance found one material positive post-baseline RCT and one material limiting RCT. PMID 42226670 reports a TBC169 synbiotic trial in middle-aged and elderly adults with chronic constipation, with symptom and quality-of-life improvements plus exploratory post-intervention microbiome composition differences. PMID 42168724 reports no gut microbiome composition change after an 8-week online-guided physical activity RCT in healthy older adults. Staged an outlook-refresh bundle that keeps the track at low-confidence human_biomarker_signal, changes momentum to mixed, and adds explicit support-map boundaries.

materiality_decision.rationale: The pass found one positive post-baseline human RCT and one limiting older-adult RCT that changed the support map and momentum language, while excluded protocol or method-limited context did not justify additional staged records.

search_log.query: microbiome OR microbiota OR probiotic OR prebiotic OR synbiotic with aging, aged, older-adult, and elderly terms; filtered to the delta window from 2026-05-11 through 2026-06-03
search_log.summary: Found two material staged sources, PMID 42226670 and PMID 42168724, plus excluded context sources PMID 42197087 and PMID 42166751.
Checked older-adult microbiome and probiotic activity; no registry update justified a public activity-only record.

excluded_sources.reason: Not staged because the aerobic exercise study in malnourished octogenarians had no control arm and high dropout, making the microbiota findings observational associations rather than causal microbiome-composition evidence.
Not staged because the BCAA supplementation paper was protocol context with gut microbiota secondary outcomes rather than results evidence.
No checked older-adult microbiome or probiotic registry update justified an activity-only public record in this delta pass.

notes: Scope was selected from the top ready surveillance queue item.
Delta window used for source discovery: 2026-05-11 through 2026-06-03, based on the current public outlook last_updated date.
PubMed searches checked microbiome, microbiota, probiotic, prebiotic, and synbiotic terms with aging, aged, older-adult, and elderly terms, filtered to publication or entry dates in the delta window.

PMID 42197087, an aerobic exercise study in malnourished octogenarians, was not staged because the authors explicitly state that the no-control design and high dropout make the microbiota results observational associations rather than causal evidence.
PMID 42166751, a BCAA supplementation protocol with gut microbiota secondary outcomes, was not staged because it is protocol context rather than microbiome-composition evidence.
ClinicalTrials.gov search was checked for older-adult microbiome/probiotic activity; no registry update in the delta pass justified a public activity-only record.
next_actions: Run evidence reviews for source_fidelity and interpretation_forecast on microbiome-composition-modulation-surveillance-2026-06-03.
If reviews pass, approve and publish the surveillance bundle.
Continue surveillance queue with ecosystem-replacement after this bundle clears editorial review.
